label: Enhanced DCA
description:
Dollar-cost-averaging enhancement: condition the timing/size of periodic buys on a dip or valuation signal instead of fixed-calendar buys. One research notebook; never implemented or validated. DCA variants usually reduce to market beta plus cash-drag timing.
First-pass study (S&P 500, in-sample): the best enhanced-DCA variant returned ~6.86% annualized vs 7.86% for lump-sum investing - i.e. it underperformed simply investing immediately. Sharpe (~0.45) and max-drawdown (~-43%, near the basic-DCA benchmark) are rough estimates.
